programme.desc: Bottom-of-the-table Wolves take three defeats on the spin to St James' Park for a clash with Eddie Howe's Newcastle, who return to action after a goalless draw with new boys Leeds. (13.09)

programme.title: PL: Everton v Aston Villa Hlts
programme.desc: With two wins from three, excitement continues to grow for David Moyes' Everton as Jack Grealish and co get another run out at the Hill Dickinson Stadium against Aston Villa. (13.09)

programme.title: PL: Bournemouth v Brighton Hlts
programme.desc: Bournemouth and Brighton return to Premier League action on the back of impressive wins prior to the break, with the Cherries beating Spurs and Brighton winning 2-1 at home to City. (13.09)

programme.title: PL: Fulham v Leeds Hlts
programme.desc: A chance for Marco Silva's Fulham to get off the mark after a winless start to the campaign. Three games into life back in the top flight, Leeds have four points to their name. (13.09)

programme.title: PL: C Palace v Sunderland Hlts
programme.desc: Riding a club record 14-game unbeaten run, Crystal Palace are tasked with a tough return to Premier League action with the visit of Régis Le Bris' new boys Sunderland. (13.09)

programme.title: SNF: West Ham v Spurs Hlts
programme.desc: A Saturday Night Football doubleheader from the capital, as West Ham and Spurs kick the night off at the London Stadium. The Irons are up and running after a 3-0 win at Forest. (13.09)

programme.title: SNF: Brentford v Chelsea Hlts
programme.desc: Unbeaten Chelsea travel to west London rivals Brentford on Saturday Night Football. Searching for momentum, Keith Andrews' Bees slipped to a second defeat in three before the break. (13.09)

programme.title: PL: Burnley v Liverpool Hlts
programme.desc: With three wins from three, a trip to Turf Moor comes next for Arne Slot's Liverpool on Super Sunday with opponents Burnley targeting the huge scalp of the Premier League champions. (14.09)

programme.title: PL: Man City v Man Utd Hlts
programme.desc: An intriguing Manchester derby sees Pep Guardiola's out-of-sorts City host Ruben Amorim's all-at-sea United, who steadied the ship before the break with a nervy win over Burnley. (14.09)

programme.title: Premier League Goals of The Season
programme.desc: A look at some of the finest strikes throughout the 2025-26 Premier League campaign.

programme.title: How Brazil Changed The Premier League
programme.desc: Brazilians were seldom seen in the Premier League's early days but things have changed. From Juninho and Gilberto Silva to Casemiro and Gabriel, this is the story of the journey.

programme.title: How Brazil Changed The Premier League
programme.desc: Brazilians were seldom seen in the Premier League's early days but things have changed. From Juninho and Gilberto Silva to Casemiro and Gabriel, this is the story of the journey.

programme.title: Premier League Years 1993/94
programme.desc: Take a look at all the memorable moments from the 1993-94 Premier League season. Manchester United were targeting their second successive title after winning the league in its first season.

programme.title: PL Retro: Man Utd v Liverpool 2009
programme.desc: Manchester United welcome Liverpool to Old Trafford in this classic Premier League match from the 2008-09 campaign. It would prove to be a memorable afternoon for the visiting side.

programme.title: PL Greatest Games:M City/L'Pool 15
programme.desc: Relive a Premier League classic, as Man City face Liverpool back in November 2015. City needed a win to go back top of the table, while Liverpool were adapting to life under Jurgen Klopp.

programme.title: PL Greatest Games:M City/L'Pool 13
programme.desc: Relive a Premier League classic, as Man City face Liverpool back in 2013. City needed three points to close the gap at the top to Man Utd, while the Reds were chasing a top four spot.

programme.title: PL Greatest Games:Man U/L'Pool '95
programme.desc: A look back at some classic matches in the Premier League. Here, Man United host Liverpool in October 1995, as Eric Cantona returned from his lengthy suspension in a thrilling 2-2 draw.

programme.title: PL Greatest Games:L'Pool/Man U '97
programme.desc: A look back at some classic matches in the Premier League. Here, Liverpool meet Manchester United at Anfield in 1997, in a match that had a happy outcome for the Red Devils.

programme.title: Best Premier League Goals 11/12
programme.desc: Relive some classic action from the Premier League. Here, a collection of some of the best goals from the 2011-2012 Premier League season.

programme.title: Best PL Goals: Newcastle/Liverpool
programme.desc: Enjoy some of the best Premier League goals from past meetings between Newcastle United and Liverpool.

programme.title: Best PL Goals: Liverpool/Man Utd
programme.desc: A look at some of the best Premier League goals scored in matches between rivals Liverpool and Manchester United.

programme.title: PL Greatest Games:Spurs/Chelsea 08
programme.desc: Relive a classic match from the Premier League. Here, Spurs and Chelsea go head-to-head at White Hart Lane in an end-to-end encounter that produced eight goals.

programme.title: PL Greatest Games:Man Utd/Man City
programme.desc: A chance to relive some classic action from the Premier League. Here, a memorable Manchester derby at Old Trafford back in the 2011-12 season that saw a six-goal Man City run rampant.

programme.title: PL Greatest Games:Man U/Arsenal 01
programme.desc: A chance to relive some classic action from the Premier League. Here, Manchester United versus Arsenal back in the 2000-01 season in a one-sided game that saw seven goals.

programme.title: PL Greatest Games: L'pool/Newc 96
programme.desc: A chance to relive a classic match from the Premier League. Here, rewind to the seven-goal thriller between Liverpool and Newcastle United at Anfield back in the 1995-96 season.

programme.title: Premier League Review
programme.desc: A look back at the weekend's action in the Premier League, featuring all of the goals, news and major talking points from around the top flight.

programme.title: Premier League Review
programme.desc: A look back at the weekend's action in the Premier League, featuring all of the goals, news and major talking points from around the top flight.

programme.title: FNF: Liverpool v Bournemouth Hlts
programme.desc: Anfield sets the stage for the 2025-26 Premier League curtain raiser as the reigning champions Liverpool get their title defence underway against Andoni Iraola's Bournemouth. (15.08)

programme.title: PL: Sunderland v West Ham Hlts
programme.desc: Régis Le Bris' Sunderland open their top-flight account at home to Graham Potter's West Ham United. The Hammers have lost more opening Premier League fixtures than any other side. (16.08)

programme.title: PL: Brighton v Fulham Hlts
programme.desc: Steering Brighton to eighth, Fabian Hurzeler's second season begins against Fulham, whose unbeaten top-flight record over the Seagulls came to an end when the two last met. (16.08)

programme.title: PL: Spurs v Burnley Hlts
programme.desc: The Thomas Frank era begins for Spurs at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium - set to stage Champions League football this season - with the Premier League visit of new boys Burnley. (16.08)

programme.title: SNF: Wolves v Man City Hlts
programme.desc: On the first Saturday Night Football of the Premier League season, Manchester City visit Vitor Pereira's Wolves. Can Pep Guardiola get City back on track after a campaign to forget? (16.08)

programme.title: PL: Nott'm Forest v Brentford Hlts
programme.desc: Starting afresh after a summer upheaval, new boss Keith Andrews takes Brentford to the City Ground on Premier League opening weekend for a Super Sunday meeting with Forest. (17.08)

programme.title: PL: Chelsea v Crystal Palace Hlts
programme.desc: The newly minted club world champions take on last weekend's Community Shield winners as Chelsea and Palace kickstart their Premier League campaigns on Super Sunday at the Bridge. (17.08)

programme.title: PL: Man Utd v Arsenal Hlts
programme.desc: On Super Sunday, United and Arsenal open their Premier League seasons at Old Trafford. With both sides boasting new forward lines, who will start their campaign off with a bang? (17.08)

programme.title: MNF: Leeds Utd v Everton Hlts
programme.desc: The first Monday Night Football of the season comes from Elland Road, where Daniel Farke's Leeds United play host to David Moyes and Everton on their Premier League return. (18.08)

programme.title: FNF: West Ham v Chelsea
programme.desc: Left wanting on opening weekend, West Ham and Chelsea target Premier League lift off on Friday Night Football. The Irons lost 3-0 at Sunderland, while Chelsea were held by Palace. (22.08)

programme.title: PL: Burnley v Sunderland Hlts
programme.desc: Up and running with a memorable 3-0 win over West Ham, Sunderland venture to fellow new boys Burnley - who began life back in the Premier League with defeat in the capital. (23.08)

programme.title: PL: Bournemouth v Wolves Hlts
programme.desc: Impressing in defeat at Anfield on opening night, Bournemouth return to the Vitality for Wolves' Premier League visit. Vitor Pereira's side lost 4-0 at home to City last week. (23.08)

programme.title: PL: Brentford v Aston Villa Hlts
programme.desc: Keith Andrews takes charge of his first Premier League home game at Brentford against Aston Villa. Ten-man Villa held Newcastle to a draw at Villa Park last weekend. (23.08)

programme.title: SNF: Arsenal v Leeds Hlts
programme.desc: Having claimed a win at Old Trafford last week, Arsenal host Leeds on Saturday Night Football. The newly promoted side kicked off their campaign with a 1-0 win over Everton. (23.08)

programme.title: PL: Everton v Brighton Hlts
programme.desc: Everton welcome Brighton to the Hill Dickinson Stadium on a historic day for the Merseyside club. The Toffees tasted defeat to newly promoted Leeds at the start of the week. (24.08)

programme.title: PL: C. Palace v Nott'm Forest Hlts
programme.desc: Crystal Palace and Nottingham Forest - two teams who dominated headlines throughout the summer - go toe to toe in a fiery Premier League clash on Super Sunday at Selhurst Park. (24.08)

programme.title: PL: Fulham v Man Utd Hlts
programme.desc: Ruben Amorim's much-improved Manchester United go in search of their first Premier League win of 2025-26 with a Super Sunday trip to face Marco Silva's Fulham at Craven Cottage. (24.08)

programme.title: MNF: Newcastle v Liverpool Hlts
programme.desc: On Monday Night Football, the transfer saga of the summer plays second fiddle as Newcastle and Liverpool enter a raucous St James' Park atmosphere for a Premier League clash. (25.08)

programme.title: Premier League Greatest Games
